---
title: About
toc: false
---

::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

![](img/Daniel%20Chen.png){width="90%"}

:::

::: {.column}

Hi I'm Daniel. I'm a Lecturer at the University of British Columbia.
I primarily teach Data Science tooling through the Statistics department,
and through the Master's of Data Science program at UBC.

Previously, I was a data science educator and developer advocate at [Posit, PBC](https://posit.co/). They are the authors of a _TON_ of [open source data science tools](https://opensource.posit.co/), some of which are used in this tutorial.

:::

:::

## Posit at SciPy 2026

:::{.callout-note}
## Meet the Shiny Team

Members of the Posit team — including Shiny for Python engineers, developers, and maintainers — will be attending SciPy 2026.

<!-- TODO: Add names, roles, and any booth/session details once confirmed -->

Come say hi and ask them anything about Shiny!
:::

## How it's made

If you're curious about the tools used to build this tutorial site:

- Quarto Website + Quarto Slides
    - <https://quarto.org/>
- Shinylive = Shiny + WebAssembly
    - <https://shiny.posit.co/py/get-started/shinylive.html>
    - Shinylive + quarto:
        - <https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive>
        - <https://quarto-ext.github.io/shinylive/>
- `brand.yml`: Specifying colors and branding
    - <https://posit-dev.github.io/brand-yml/>
